src/flit/app

  • Index
Search:
Group by:
  • Imports
  • Procs
        runApp
      • runApp(w: Widget; config = defaultDesktopConfig())
  • Exports

App entry. The user calls runApp(myRootWidget) and flit picks the right platform backend at compile time based on which target (and which -d:flag) the program was built for.

How the platform is chosen:

nim c     examples/counter/main.nim           # desktop (SDL2)
nim c -d:android examples/counter/main.nim    # android (SDL2 mobile)
nim c -d:ios     examples/counter/main.nim    # iOS    (SDL2 mobile)
nim js  examples/counter/web.nim              # web (HTMLCanvas)
nim c -d:flitEmbedded examples/embed.nim      # framebuffer / kiosk

The exported runApp proc's signature varies slightly per backend: the desktop runner takes a DesktopWindowConfig, the web runner takes a canvas element id, the mobile runner takes a title/font path, and the embedded runner takes width/height and a pixel-flush callback.

Imports

foundation/widget, platform/desktop/runner

Procs

proc runApp(w: Widget; config = defaultDesktopConfig()) {.
    ...raises: [PixieError, Exception, KeyError],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect, WriteIOEffect, TimeEffect],
    forbids: [].}

Desktop entry point (macOS / Linux / Windows).

Inputs:

  • w: root widget. Required.
  • config: a DesktopWindowConfig controlling window size, title, vsync, high-DPI, and font path. Defaults to a 1024x768 resizable vsync'd window with auto-discovered system font.

Effect: blocks until the user closes the window. Drives the SDL2 event loop, runs frames, and dispatches input to gesture detectors.

Exports

DesktopWindowConfig, runDesktop, defaultDesktopConfig

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-09 05:33:51 UTC
